---
title: "TidyTuesday"
author: "Mark Deming"
date: "1/27/2026"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)

# Packages
pacman::p_load(here, tidyverse, usethis, gitcreds, tidytuesdayR, ggridges, extrafont)

# Open GitHub token page in browser
#usethis::create_github_token()

# Store the PAT securely
#gitcreds::gitcreds_set()

# Grab TT data
tt <- tidytuesdayR::tt_load("2026-01-27")

# Find font
#extrafont::font_import()
extrafont::loadfonts(device = "pdf")
fonts()

# Pull data from TT
companies <- tt$companies
legal_nature <- tt$legal_nature
qualifications <- tt$qualifications
size <- tt$size

# Designate a color palette
# Tableau palette with 14 colors
pal14 <- ggthemes::tableau_color_pal("Tableau 20")(14)


# Density ridges plot by legal_nature
p <-
companies %>%
  filter(capital_stock != max(capital_stock)) %>%
  group_by(legal_nature) %>%
  filter(n() > 10) %>%
  mutate(median_stock = median(capital_stock, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = log10(capital_stock), y = reorder(legal_nature, -median_stock), fill = legal_nature)) +
  ggridges::geom_density_ridges(alpha = .7, show.legend = FALSE) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(4.5, 12.5),
                    breaks = seq(5, 12, 1),
                     labels = function(x)
    scales::label_dollar(
      prefix = "R$ ",
      scale_cut = scales::cut_short_scale(),
      accuracy = 1
    )(10^x),
                    expand = c(0,0)) +
  scale_y_discrete(expand = expansion(mult = c(0.02, 0.15))) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = pal14) +
  labs(x = "\nDeclared capital (R$)",
       y = "",
       title = "Distribution of Declared Capital among Brazilian Companies by Legal Nature",
       subtitle = "In Brazilian reais (R$)\n") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.major.x = element_line(linetype = "dotted", color = "gray70"),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        plot.title.position = "plot",
        axis.text = element_text(color = "black"),
        text = element_text(family = "Raleway"),
        plot.margin = margin(t = 10, unit = "pt"))

#.SF Compact
#Raleway
#DIN Condensed
#STIX Two Text

ggsave(here("2026.01.27", "density_ridges_plot.png"),
       height = 6,
       width = 12,
       device = "png",
       type = "cairo")


```
